macros.token: #if str($output_format.format_mode) == "0" or str($output_format.format_mode) == "2" or str($output_format.format_mode) == "4" #if $output_format.format_fields --format-output '${ ",".join($output_format.format_fields) }' #end if #end if --format-mode $output_format.format_mode

param.label: Input data type
param.help: Controls how input sequences are interpreted. Applies to both query and target. Auto-detect is recommended. See the Search types section in the help below for cross-type search guidance.

param.label: Scale composition bias correction
param.help: Lower values increase correction strength for biased amino acid compositions.

param.label: Maximal allowed difference between score values before alignment is truncated
param.help: Terminates extension when score drops by this value. Lower values produce shorter alignments.

param.name: target_source_select
param.label: Target sequences source
param.help: Choose a FASTA file from your history or a pre-indexed database installed on the server.

param.name: target_fasta
param.label: Target FASTA file
param.help: FASTA file containing target sequences to search against.

param.name: mmseqs2_db_select
param.label: MMseqs2 database
param.help: Select a pre-indexed MMseqs2 database installed by the server administrator.

param.label: Coverage mode
param.help: Determines how coverage is calculated from aligned residues relative to query and/or target length.
param.option: Target seq. length has to be at least x% of query length
Query seq. length has to be at least x% of target length
Short seq. needs to be at least x% of the other seq. length

param.name: cov
param.label: Minimum coverage fraction of aligned residues
param.help: Fraction of aligned residues required (0.0-1.0). Higher values require longer alignments.

param.label: Minimum sequence identity
param.help: Only report hits with sequence identity above this threshold (0.0-1.0).

param.name: evalue
param.label: E-value threshold
param.help: List matches below this E-value

param.label: Artificially add entries of queries with themselves (for clustering)
param.help: Useful when clustering to ensure each sequence matches itself.

param.name: kmer_length
param.label: k-mer length
param.help: (0: automatically set to optimum)

param.label: Mask sequences in k-mer stage
param.help: Low complexity regions can cause spurious hits. Masking filters them during k-mer matching.

param.label: Mask sequences if probability is above threshold
param.help: Regions with compositional bias probability above this threshold are masked.

param.label: Mask lower case letters
param.help: Controls whether lower case letters in input sequences are treated as masked regions.

param.label: Repeat letters that occur > threshold in a row
param.help: Mask stretches of N characters longer than this threshold. Set 0 to disable.

param.label: Target search mode
param.help: Similar k-mer mode finds more remote homologs but is slower.

param.label: Maximum results per query sequence allowed to pass the prefilter
param.help: Affects sensitivity

param.label: Split input into N equally distributed chunks
param.help: 0: set the best split automatically

param.label: Split mode
param.help: Controls how the database is split for parallel processing. Auto mode selects based on available memory.

param.label: Use ungapped diagonal scoring during prefilter
param.help: Speeds up prefiltering by scoring ungapped diagonals. Disable for very sensitive searches.

param.label: Extract only exact k-mers for matching
param.help: When enabled (1), only exact k-mer matches are used. Faster but less sensitive.

param.label: Accept only matches with ungapped alignment score above threshold
param.help: Prefilter candidates below this ungapped alignment score are discarded.

param.name: convertalis
param.label: Add backtrace string
param.help: Convert to alignments with mmseqs convertalis module

param.label: Alignment output mode
param.help: Controls how much alignment detail is computed and stored. Higher modes are slower but provide more information.

param.label: Double the (nucleotide) query sequence during the scoring process
param.help: Allow wrapped diagonal scoring around end and start

param.label: Minimum alignment length
param.help: Discard alignments shorter than this many residues. Set 0 for no minimum.

param.label: Sequence identity mode
param.help: Denominator for computing sequence identity: alignment length, shorter sequence, or longer sequence.

param.label: Number of alternative alignments to show
param.help: Show additional sub-optimal alignments for each query-target pair. Set 0 for best only.

param.label: Score bias when computing Smith-Waterman alignment
param.help: Added to the substitution score. Positive values favor longer alignments.

param.label: Compute more conservative, shorter alignments
param.help: Scores and E-values not changed

param.label: Additional bias when computing realignment
param.help: Additional score bias applied during the realignment step.

param.label: Maximum number of results to return in realignment
param.help: Default (2147483647) means no limit

param.label: Weight of backtrace correlation score that is added to the alignment score
param.help: Weight for backtrace correlation score. Set 0 to disable.

param.label: Alignment mode
param.help: Controls how much alignment detail is computed. Higher modes provide more information but are slower.

param.label: Maximum rejected alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped
param.help: Stop alignment calculation after this many consecutive rejected hits per query.

param.label: Maximum accepted alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped
param.help: Stop alignment calculation after this many accepted hits per query.

param.label: Cluster mode
param.help: Algorithm for resolving clusters from pairwise similarities. Set-Cover maximizes cluster sizes.

param.label: Maximum depth of breadth first search in connected component clustering
param.help: Only applies to Connected component mode (mode 1). Limits search depth.

param.label: Type of score used for clustering
param.help: Metric used to determine cluster membership.

param.label: Weight threshold used for cluster priorization
param.help: Sequences with weight above this threshold are prioritized as cluster centers.

param.label: Number of k-mers per sequence
param.help: More k-mers per sequence increases sensitivity but uses more memory.

param.label: Shift k-mer hash initialization
param.help: Shift value for k-mer hash function initialization.

param.label: Include only extendable
param.help: Only include k-mer matches that can be extended into longer alignments.

param.label: Skip k-mers occurring multiple times (>=2)
param.help: Skip k-mers that appear in multiple sequences to reduce noise from common motifs.

param.label: Rescore diagonals with
param.help: Method used to rescore candidate hits from the prefilter stage.
param.option: Longest alignment fulfilling window quality criterion

param.label: Shuffle input database
param.help: Randomize input order to improve load balancing during parallel processing.

param.label: Numeric ids in index file are offset by this value
param.help: Add this offset to all numeric sequence IDs in the output.

param.label: Maximum sequence length
param.help: Sequences longer than this are skipped. Increase for very long sequences.

param.label: Filter hits by seq.id. and coverage
param.help: Apply sequence identity and coverage thresholds as post-filters on results.

param.label: Sort results
param.help: Sorting by E-value is useful for downstream analysis but adds processing time.

param.label: Search type
param.help: Controls the alignment algorithm. Auto detects from input sequences. See the help section below for details on each mode and cross-type searches.

param.label: Search type
param.help: Controls the alignment algorithm. See the help section below for details on each mode and cross-type searches.

param.label: Output format
param.help: BLAST-TAB produces a 12-column tab-separated table matching BLAST -outfmt 6. SAM is compatible with samtools.

param.name: format_fields
param.label: Custom output columns
param.help: Select columns to include in output. Leave empty for default (query,target,fident,alnlen,mismatch,gapopen,qstart,qend,tstart,tend,evalue,bits).

validator.message: Select at least one output file

param.label: Mask query sequence of profile using tantan
param.help: Masks low-complexity regions in profile queries to reduce spurious matches.

param.label: Include sequences matches with E-value threshold into the profile
param.help: Sequences with E-value below this threshold are included when building the profile.

param.label: Use global sequence weighting for profile calculation
param.help: When enabled, uses global sequence weights instead of position-based weights.

param.label: Filter MSA
param.help: Filters redundant sequences from the MSA before profile computation.

param.label: Only filter MSAs with more than N sequences, 0 always filters
param.help: Skip MSA filtering when the MSA has fewer sequences than this threshold.

param.label: Reduce redundancy of output MSA using max. pairwise sequence identity
param.help: Remove sequences from the MSA that are more similar than this threshold to another sequence.

param.label: Reduce diversity of output MSAs using min. seq. identity with query sequences [0.0,1.0]
param.help: Alternatively, can be a list of multiple thresholds: e.g. 0.15,0.30,0.50

validator.message: Must be a float or comma-separated list of floats (e.g. 0.15,0.30,0.50)

param.label: Reduce diversity of output MSAs using min. score per aligned residue with query sequences
param.help: Remove sequences from the MSA with a score per column below this threshold.

param.label: Filter output MSAs using min. fraction of query residues covered by matched sequences
param.help: Remove sequences from the MSA that cover fewer query residues than this fraction.

param.label: Filter MSAs by selecting most diverse set of sequences, keeping at least this many seqs in each MSA block of length 50
param.help: Selects the most diverse sequences from each MSA block for profile building.

param.label: Pseudo count mode
param.help: Method for adding pseudo counts to the profile. Context-specific is more accurate but slower.

param.label: Exhaustive search
param.help: Disables prefiltering and aligns all database sequences. Very slow but maximally sensitive.

param.label: Efficient search for LCA candidates
param.help: Optimizes the search strategy for lowest common ancestor computation in taxonomy workflows.

param.label: E-value threshold used for query ORF prefiltering
param.help: Pre-screen ORFs using this E-value threshold before the main taxonomy search.

param.label: Sensitivity used for query ORF prefiltering
param.help: Sensitivity used for the ORF pre-screening step.

param.label: LCA mode
param.help: Single search: fast, one search pass. Approximate 2bLCA: more accurate, two-pass approach. Top hit: use only the best hit.

param.label: Taxonomy output mode
param.help: Controls whether LCA taxonomy, raw alignments, or both are included in the output.

param.label: Minimal fraction of agreement among taxonomically assigned sequences of a set
param.help: Minimum fraction of hits that must agree on a taxon for it to be assigned.

param.label: Mode of assigning weights to compute majority
param.help: How hits are weighted when computing the majority taxon. Log E-value weights give more influence to better hits.

param.label: Taxonomy lineage
param.help: When enabled, the full taxonomic lineage is appended to each result row.

param.label: Comma separated list of ignored taxa in LCA computation
param.help: Exclude these taxon IDs from the LCA computation (e.g. unclassified taxa).

param.label: Taxonomy ID, possibly multiple values separated by ','
param.help: Restrict the search to only these taxon IDs. Prefix with ! to exclude.

param.label: Allow deletions in a MSA
param.help: When enabled, ORFs containing deletions in the MSA are still considered.

param.label: Minimum codon number in open reading frames
param.help: ORFs with fewer codons than this are discarded.

param.label: Maximum codon number in open reading frames
param.help: ORFs with more codons than this are discarded.

param.label: Maximum number of codons with gaps or unknown residues before an open reading frame is rejected
param.help: Default (2147483647) means no limit

param.label: Contig start can be
param.help: Whether contigs can start with an incomplete ORF (no start codon at sequence beginning).

param.label: Contig end can be
param.help: Whether contigs can end with an incomplete ORF (no stop codon at sequence end).

param.label: ORF fragment can be
param.help: Controls how ORF boundaries are defined relative to start and stop codons.
param.option: From last encountered start to stop (no start in the middle)

param.label: Comma-separated list of frames on the forward strand to be extracted
param.help: Reading frames to extract on the forward strand (1, 2, 3).

validator.message: Must be a comma-separated list of frame numbers (1, 2, 3)

param.label: Comma-separated list of frames on the reverse strand to be extracted
param.help: Reading frames to extract on the reverse strand (1, 2, 3).

validator.message: Must be a comma-separated list of frame numbers (1, 2, 3)

param.label: Translation table
param.help: NCBI genetic code table for translating nucleotide sequences to amino acids.
param.option: The Mold, Protozoan, and Coelenterate Mitochondrial Code and the Mycoplasma/Spiroplasma Code
The Ciliate, Dasycladacean and Hexamita Nuclear Code
The Echinoderm and Flatworm Mitochondrial Code
The Bacterial, Archaeal and Plant Plastid Code
Candidate Division SR1 and Gracilibacteria Code

param.label: Translate ORF to amino acid
param.help: When enabled, extracted ORFs are translated to amino acid sequences.

param.label: Use all alternatives for a start codon in the genetic table, if false - only ATG (AUG)
param.help: Use alternative start codons defined in the genetic code table, not just ATG.

param.label: Use the first sequence of the clustering result as representative sequence
param.help: By default, the longest sequence is the representative. Enable to use the first instead.

param.label: Select a target column, 0 if no target id exists
param.help: Column index in the result database to use as target identifier.

param.label: Replace DB ID by its corresponding Full Header
param.help: Output the complete FASTA header instead of just the sequence identifier.

param.label: Index sequences source
param.help: Source of sequences when using precomputed indices.

macros.token:
**MMseqs2: ultra fast and sensitive sequence search and clustering suite**
MMseqs2 (Many-against-Many sequence searching) is a software suite to search and cluster huge protein and nucleotide sequence sets. It can run 10000 times faster than BLAST. At 100 times its speed it achieves almost the same sensitivity. It can perform profile searches with the same sensitivity as PSI-BLAST at over 400 times its speed.
**The MMseqs2 ecosystem**
MMseqs2 provides a family of easy-to-use tools for common sequence analysis tasks. Each tool accepts FASTA/FASTQ files directly and produces standard output formats:
- **easy-search** -- Sensitive homology search (replaces BLAST). Use for general-purpose searching. - **easy-linsearch** -- Fast linear-time search. Use for very large databases when high-identity hits are expected. - **easy-cluster** -- Sensitive cascaded clustering. Use for smaller datasets or when maximum sensitivity is needed. - **easy-linclust** -- Linear-time clustering. Use for very large datasets (millions of sequences). - **easy-rbh** -- Reciprocal best hit search. Use for ortholog detection between two species or gene sets. - **easy-taxonomy** -- Taxonomy assignment via LCA. Use for metagenomic classification.
.. image:: $PATH_TO_IMAGES/mmseqs2_tool_selection.png :alt: MMseqs2 tool selection guide
**Key concepts**
- **Sensitivity (-s parameter)**: Controls the speed/sensitivity tradeoff (1.0 = fast, 7.0 = BLAST-like sensitivity). Higher values let more candidates through the prefilter stage. - **Coverage (-c and --cov-mode)**: Minimum fraction of aligned residues required. Coverage mode controls whether query, target, or both lengths are considered. - **Sequence identity (--min-seq-id)**: Minimum sequence identity threshold for reporting hits or forming clusters (0.0-1.0). - **Search types**: Protein-vs-protein (BLASTP), translated nucleotide-vs-protein (BLASTX), protein-vs-translated nucleotide (TBLASTN), nucleotide-vs-nucleotide (BLASTN), and translated-vs-translated (TBLASTX) are all supported and auto-detected from input types.

-----
More information is available in the `MMseqs2 User Guide <https://github.com/soedinglab/MMseqs2/wiki>`_.
